zh.title: 以太坊/美元技术分析：震荡偏强整理上行
zh.content:
当前以太坊价格围绕1720上方震荡整理，短线维持偏强结构，整体仍运行在20日均线上方，显示多头趋势未破坏，但上涨动能略有放缓进入盘整阶段

MACD指标维持正值12.41，动能柱仍偏多头，短期买盘占优但信号线未补充略显滞后，市场处于趋势延续与震荡修正并存状态中枢震荡延续中

上方阻力关注1723.7历史高点，若有效突破或打开1735至1750空间，下方支撑位于1716及1686一带20日均线，回调仍可逢低布局

交易策略建议以回踩支撑分批低吸为主，避免追高操作，若价格稳站1723上方可轻仓顺势跟多，跌破1716则需谨慎并等待1686区域再观察反弹信号

en.title: Ethereum vs US Dollar Technical Analysis
en.content:
ETH/USD is currently trading at 1721.92, showing a marginal 0.25% daily gain and remaining in a tight consolidation range. Price action is compressing between 1715.98 and 1723.72, indicating low volatility and equilibrium between buyers and sellers. Despite the sideways movement, the pair holds above the 20-day SMA at 1686.34, suggesting the broader short-term trend remains mildly bullish with buyers defending higher lows and maintaining structural strength.

Momentum indicators suggest a cautiously positive bias. Although RSI (14) is not available, the MACD reading at 12.41951 indicates underlying bullish momentum, supported by price stability above the SMA(20). The lack of a visible signal line limits full confirmation, but current positioning implies that momentum has not yet turned bearish. Overall momentum appears neutral-to-bullish, with consolidation potentially acting as a continuation base rather than reversal.

Immediate support is seen at 1715.98, followed by a stronger zone around 1710 and the 20-day SMA near 1686.34, which acts as a key structural floor. On the upside, resistance is located at 1723.72, with a breakout potentially opening the path toward 1735 and then 1750. The tight range suggests an imminent volatility expansion once either boundary is decisively breached, likely defining the next directional move.

Trading strategies favor range-bound discipline until confirmation of breakout direction. Short-term traders may consider buying near support levels with tight stops below 1710, targeting a retest of resistance at 1723–1735. Alternatively, a clean breakout above 1723.72 could signal continuation momentum entries. Risk management remains critical given compressed volatility, as false breakouts are common in low-volume consolidation phases like the current structure.
